comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: OBSOLETED by the retire-auto-lander migration (epic d4de / retire-code story d0e5): the owner decided (2026-07-13) to drop the bot entirely and land via Gerrit Rebase-If-Necessary + agent Submit. The code carrying this bug (infra/autolander/) is being DELETED, which is this bug's resolution. Close this as obsolete when d0e5 lands; no separate fix will be written.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

Observed LIVE on the deployed bot (2026-07-13): another agent set Autosubmit+1 on a 2-change stack (#644,#645, 'speed up gerrit-verify'). Both were LLM+1/Verified+1/submittable at selection, but main advanced before the bot's submit, so ancestor-atomic submit hit Gerrit 409 'requires fast-forward'. The bot then **emitted AUTOLANDER_ERROR every poll cycle in an infinite loop** (dozens of identical errors), never converging: it did NOT successfully rebase-on-behalf, and it did NOT clear the stack's Autosubmit (both changes STILL show Autosubmit=+1), so it re-selected the same stale stack forever. This floods the rebar-autolander-errors alarm and starves the serial queue (nothing else lands). Mitigated by engaging the S5d emergency-stop sentinel (bot paused).

## Likely root cause(s)
1. The SUBMIT-first design expects a submit-409 (not-ff) to trigger a rebase-onto-tip (drive_to_submit). For this stale STACK, that rebase path did not run (or failed) and fell through to a loud AUTOLANDER_ERROR + re-select.
2. The hand-back that should remove Autosubmit on a failed drive did not clear the votes — plausibly because the votes were cast by ANOTHER account and RebarBotNava lacks permission to remove another user's label vote (Gerrit label-removal perms), so the 'hand back = remove Autosubmit' step silently fails and the change stays selectable. (Related to but distinct from 0d3e, which is about Autosubmit being dropped across the bot's OWN rebase.)

## Reproduction Steps
1. Create a 2+ change stack, get it submittable (LLM+1/Verified+1).
2. Advance main so the stack is no longer a fast-forward.
3. Set Autosubmit+1 on the stack (as a non-bot account).
4. Observe the bot select it, submit → 409, and loop on AUTOLANDER_ERROR without rebasing or clearing Autosubmit.

Expected: on a submit-409 the bot rebases-on-behalf + re-CIs + submits; if it cannot (conflict / can't clear Autosubmit), it hands back ONCE (removes its selectability) and moves on — never an infinite error loop.
Actual: infinite AUTOLANDER_ERROR loop; stack stuck; queue starved.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A submit-409 (not-ff) on a selected stack deterministically routes to rebase-onto-tip (rebase-on-behalf) + fresh-Verified await + re-submit, OR a single clean hand-back — never a repeating error loop.
- [ ] The hand-back reliably makes the change non-selectable even when the Autosubmit vote was cast by another account (handle the label-removal permission case, or exclude a just-failed change from re-selection via the S3 marker/backoff).
- [ ] A regression test simulates the stale-stack submit-409 and asserts bounded outcomes (rebase-and-reland OR one hand-back), asserting NO unbounded AUTOLANDER_ERROR repetition.
- [ ] [operator-attested] After the fix deploys, a stale Autosubmit'd stack lands (or hands back once) without looping, and the alarm stays OK.
compiled_state.title: Auto-lander loops on AUTOLANDER_ERROR for a stale submittable stack (submit-409 not rebased; hand-back doesn't clear others' Autosubmit)
